description: Library chart templates that ship with warphead.dev - automatically loaded on app initialization

charts.description: Vertical bar chart for comparing values across categories
charts.chart_code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Plot, BarY } from 'svelteplot';
  export let data: any[] = [];
  export let x: string = 'category';
  export let y: string = 'value';
  export let fill: string = '#6366F1';
  export let xLabel: string = '';
  export let yLabel: string = '';
</script>

<Plot
  x={{ label: xLabel }}
  y={{ grid: true, label: yLabel }}
  height={400}
>
  <BarY
    {data}
    x={x}
    y={y}
    {fill}
  />
</Plot>

charts.description: Line chart for showing trends over time or continuous data
charts.chart_code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Plot, Line } from 'svelteplot';
  export let data: any[] = [];
  export let x: string = 'date';
  export let y: string = 'value';
  export let stroke: string = '#6366F1';
  export let strokeWidth: number = 2;
  export let xLabel: string = '';
  export let yLabel: string = '';
</script>

<Plot
  x={{ label: xLabel }}
  y={{ grid: true, label: yLabel }}
  height={400}
>
  <Line
    {data}
    x={x}
    y={y}
    {stroke}
    {strokeWidth}
  />
</Plot>

charts.description: Scatter plot for showing relationships between two variables
charts.chart_code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Plot, Dot } from 'svelteplot';
  export let data: any[] = [];
  export let x: string = 'x';
  export let y: string = 'y';
  export let fill: string = '#6366F1';
  export let r: number = 3;
  export let xLabel: string = '';
  export let yLabel: string = '';
</script>

<Plot
  x={{ grid: true, label: xLabel }}
  y={{ grid: true, label: yLabel }}
  height={400}
>
  <Dot
    {data}
    x={x}
    y={y}
    {fill}
    {r}
  />
</Plot>

charts.description: Area chart for showing cumulative trends over time
charts.chart_code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Plot, AreaY } from 'svelteplot';
  export let data: any[] = [];
  export let x: string = 'date';
  export let y: string = 'value';
  export let fill: string = '#6366F1';
  export let fillOpacity: number = 0.3;
  export let xLabel: string = '';
  export let yLabel: string = '';
</script>

<Plot
  x={{ label: xLabel }}
  y={{ grid: true, label: yLabel }}
  height={400}
>
  <AreaY
    {data}
    x={x}
    y={y}
    {fill}
    {fillOpacity}
  />
</Plot>

charts.description: Heatmap for visualizing data density or values across two dimensions
charts.chart_code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Plot, Cell } from 'svelteplot';
  export let data: any[] = [];
  export let x: string = 'x';
  export let y: string = 'y';
  export let fill: string = 'value';
  export let xLabel: string = '';
  export let yLabel: string = '';
</script>

<Plot
  x={{ label: xLabel }}
  y={{ label: yLabel }}
  color={{ scheme: 'blues' }}
  height={400}
>
  <Cell
    {data}
    x={x}
    y={y}
    {fill}
  />
</Plot>

charts.description: Simple data table for displaying tabular data
charts.chart_code:
{#each displayColumns as column} {/each} {#each data as row} {#each displayColumns as column} {/each} {/each}
{column}
{row[column] ?? '-'}

charts.description: Single value KPI display with optional trend indicator
charts.chart_code:
{label}
{formatValue(displayValue)}
{#if trend !== null}
0} class:text-red-600={trend < 0} class:text-slate-500={trend === 0}> {trend > 0 ? '↑' : trend < 0 ? '↓' : '→'} {Math.abs(trend).toFixed(1)}%
{/if}
